job_description:
About the Job

As a specialist in electronics and software for the past 20 years, in-tech is a dynamic, fast-growing engineering company headquartered in Munich, Germany, with 2350+ employees globally across 20 project locations in 8 countries. We develop innovative engineering solutions for the automotive, rail, aerospace, defence, and industrial sectors. Since 2024, in-tech has become a subsidiary of Infosys Ltd, enhancing our ability to deliver comprehensive development and digitalisation services with greater offshore capabilities.

Our culture emphasizes flexibility, work-life balance, and the "Orange Spirit"—a vibrant, team-oriented ethos rooted in collaboration, creativity, and positivity.

Responsibilities

  • Design and develop high-quality label templates using Nice Label Designer and Power Forms.
  • Create barcode, RFID, variable data, and compliance labels.
  • Implement dynamic elements like conditional objects and database-driven values.
  • Integrate Nice Label with ERP, WMS, and MES systems.
  • Work with REST APIs and database connections for automated label printing.
  • Configure and maintain Nice Label LMS and Control Centre.
  • Automate printing workflows using Nice Label Automation Builder.
  • Manage version control, label approval workflows, and deployment.
  • Ensure compliance with GS1, UDI, GHS, FDA, and ISO standards.
  • Test, validate, and deploy label designs in production.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Production, Quality, IT, Logistics).
  • Provide troubleshooting, support, and training for label printing and Nice Label tools.

Requirements

  • 4–6 years of label design experience, with 3+ years using LoftWare Nice Label.
  • Strong knowledge of Nice Label Designer, Power Forms, and Automation Builder.
  • Experience with Label Management System (LMS) and Control Centre.
  • Familiarity with barcode standards (Code128, QR, Data Matrix, GS1).
  • Database integration experience (SQL Server, Oracle).
  • Proficiency in REST API integration and web services.
  • Knowledge of network printers (Zebra, SATO, Honeywell).
  • Understanding of manufacturing/supply chain labeling processes.
  • Experience with Windows Server, IIS, Active Directory, and basic networking.
  • Familiarity with monitoring/ITSM tools (ServiceNow, Jira).
  • Strong troubleshooting and root cause analysis skills.

Apply with us

If you’re passionate about joining a growing, dynamic team with a positive culture, apply now! We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. Submit your resume to india.talent@in-tech.com.

job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function, and this role owns the written voice of the company end-to-end. As a technology services firm with over 15 years of delivery experience, we work with mid-market and enterprise clients in the US and UK, specializing in AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernization, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This is a senior writing role focused on operationalizing and enforcing the company’s written voice across all content. It is not a content management or social media role. Key responsibilities include: - All long-form content: Pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK. - Leadership voice content: Writing in the voice of senior leadership for professional platforms. - Content quality gate: Ensuring all published content meets brand standards and passes quality checks. - Content briefs: Creating clear briefs for junior team members to produce first drafts without ambiguity. - AI tool integration: Using AI as a productivity layer, not a substitute for original thinking.

What You Will Not Own

  • SEO keyword selection (handled by the SEO Specialist)
  • Social media scheduling (managed by another team member)
  • Visual design (owned by the Visual Designer)

Experience: - Minimum 3–5 years writing B2B content (strong candidates have 5–6 years) - Proven content that ranked on Google - Experience writing in voices other than your own (executives, founders, senior professionals) - Working-level SEO knowledge (understands search intent) - Experience with AI writing tools and their limitations - Demonstrable content outcomes (traffic, leads, engagement)

Background: - Technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred

Writing Ability: - Writes with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries - Explains complex technical concepts in plain language without losing credibility - Writes concisely, avoiding filler and unnecessary qualification - Adapts to different voices authentically

Contact: Apply by sharing your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression, and then owns every asset that follows.

The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up. There is no brand system to inherit, no template library to use, and no consistent visual identity to maintain. What exists is a clear strategic direction, a defined audience of senior technology and operations leaders, and a marketing team building the content engine around it. Your job is to give that engine a visual identity it deserves.

The first 30 days of this role have one priority: build the brand system. Everything else, including LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates, is built from that foundation. A designer who wants to jump straight into production before the system exists is not the right fit.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is sceptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system, including colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and the documentation that makes it usable by the whole team
  • All marketing and content visual assets, including LinkedIn carousels, single-image posts, social graphics, infographics, and diagrams
  • Paid advertising creatives, including Google Display and LinkedIn Ads, designed to be tested and iterated
  • Landing page visual design, including layout, components, and conversion-focused structure, handed to the development team for build
  • Email visual templates, presentation templates, and sales deck templates
  • A template library that enables other team members to produce on-brand assets independently
  • Design consistency auditing, including periodic review of all published assets to ensure alignment with the brand system
  • Secondary responsibility: light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use, supporting other team members

What You Will Not Own

  • What an asset communicates. The Content and Thought Leadership Specialist owns the message, argument, and information hierarchy. You may suggest improvements in structure, but content decisions remain with them
  • Copywriting. The Content Specialist writes the words; you structure them visually
  • Website development. You design the pages; the development team builds them
  • Content strategy. You execute what is briefed, not decide what gets created
  • Brand positioning. The Marketing Operating System defines how 2Base communicates; you interpret it visually

Requirements

Experience

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role, preferably in technology, SaaS, or professional services
  • Experience building a brand system or significantly evolving one, not just producing isolated assets
  • Experience designing for LinkedIn, including carousel formats, document posts, and single-image feed content
  • Experience creating paid ad creatives that have been executed, tested, and iterated
  • Experience designing landing pages with an understanding of conversion principles
  • Comfort with basic video editing for social and web content, including reels, short-form YouTube, and simple website assets
  • Proficiency in Figma, which is non-negotiable, and Adobe Creative Suite including Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects

Design Thinking

  • Portfolio demonstrates restraint through clean, structured, and purposeful design
  • Ability to explain the reasoning behind key design decisions
  • Designs with a specific audience in mind, not for general appeal
  • Strong understanding and consistent application of typography as a functional tool
  • Ability to produce work that appears credible to a senior B2B audience at first glance

Mindset

  • Able to work effectively from structured briefs
  • Works at speed without compromising quality
  • Treats design as a communication tool rather than purely creative expression
  • Open to feedback and able to give it constructively

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, event design, or campaigns with no B2B relevance
  • Designers who can describe visuals but cannot justify their decisions
  • Candidates without experience building a brand system
  • Designers who overcomplicate visuals with excessive fonts, colours, styles, or effects
  • Candidates who require complete creative freedom and struggle with structured briefs

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Lead the creation of 2Base's visual brand system and design B2B marketing assets. Requires 3-5 years of experience in technology or SaaS design.

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.
  • Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications.
  • Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity.
  • Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity.
  • Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams.
  • Understanding of API behaviours, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions.
  • Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions.
  • Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage.
  • Demonstrated ownership of requirements end-to-end, ensuring developer-ready and testable outputs.
  • Not suitable for UI-only thinkers, passive communicators, template-based writers, or those relying on AI for answers.
  • Success is defined by clear, testable requirements, zero rework, strong cross-team alignment, and confident communication.
  • Candidates must align with 2Base’s structured thinking, clarity-driven culture, and transformation of the BA function.

Minimum Qualifications

Brief Description

We are seeking a seasoned Technical Delivery Manager with 7+ years of experience to oversee the successful delivery of complex technical projects and solutions. The ideal candidate will have a strong technology background, proven project delivery expertise, and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams. This role involves end-to-end ownership of project delivery, ensuring alignment with business goals, managing stakeholder expectations, and driving technical and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

  • Project Delivery Management: Plan, execute, and deliver multiple technical projects successfully. Manage project scope, timelines, budgets, risks, and quality. Ensure adherence to Agile or Waterfall methodologies based on project needs.
  • Team Leadership: Lead and mentor cross-functional technical teams. Foster collaboration, innovation, and a high-performance culture. Conduct regular performance reviews and support team development.
  • Stakeholder Engagement: Act as a bridge between clients, business stakeholders, and technical teams. Communicate project progress, risks, and mitigation plans clearly and effectively.
  • Technical Oversight: Work closely with architects and engineers to define technical solutions. Provide inputs on architecture, design, and implementation strategies.
  • Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to improve delivery processes, tools, and frameworks. Implement best practices in software development and project delivery.
  • Risk & Issue Management: Proactively identify delivery risks and define mitigation strategies. Resolve technical and operational challenges in a timely manner.
  • Customer Focus: Ensure high customer satisfaction by delivering quality solutions aligned with business requirements.

Preferred Skills

  • Experience: Minimum 7+ years in technology delivery and project management roles. Proven track record of delivering medium to large-scale projects. Experience working with cross-functional and distributed teams.
  • Technical Expertise: Strong understanding of modern software development practices. Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP. Familiarity with DevOps concepts and CI/CD pipelines. Knowledge of at least one programming language (Java, Python, .NET) is an advantage.
  • Project Management: Hands-on experience with Agile, Scrum, Kanban, or traditional project management models. Certifications like PMP, CSM, or equivalent will be an added advantage.
  • Leadership & Communication: Strong people management and decision-making skills. Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management abilities.
  • Qualification: B.Tech / B.E / MCA / M.Tech in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

Interested candidates may forward their detailed resumes to recruiter@kenland.in along with their notice period, current CTC details.

About Lean Transition Solutions (LTS)

LTS is a global Operational Excellence and Lean Industry 4.0 software company headquartered in the UK. For over a decade, we’ve helped large organizations turn strategy into action by combining Lean thinking, leadership principles, and modern, scalable technology. Trusted by leading global organizations, we support sustainable, people-centric transformation at scale. With teams across India, Europe, the US, the Middle East, and Mexico, we operate as one connected global team. At LTS, we believe technology strengthens people, enabling them to do their best work.

Location: Technopark Phase 1, Trivandrum

Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced and motivated Scrum Master to support our Agile teams in delivering high-quality products through effective collaboration and continuous improvement. The ideal candidate will guide teams in adopting Agile best practices, facilitate Scrum ceremonies, and help remove obstacles that impact delivery. This role is key to fostering a transparent, productive, and high-performing Agile environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Facilitate all Scrum ceremonies: Sprint Scope and Planning, Daily Stand-ups, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives.
  • Coach and mentor team members and stakeholders on Agile/Scrum best practices.
  • Track and communicate team velocity, sprint progress, and delivery metrics to stakeholders.
  • Identify and proactively remove blockers and impediments that hinder team progress.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, transparency, and continuous improvement.
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders and internal teams to ensure the product backlog or task list is clearly defined, prioritized, and aligned with business goals.
  • Protect the Scrum team from external disruptions and scope changes during active sprints to maintain focus and delivery commitments.
  • Support the organization’s Agile adoption and transformation by promoting Agile values, principles, and best practices across teams.
  • Facilitate healthy and constructive conflict resolution within the team, encouraging open communication and collaboration.
  • Assist in resource planning and allocation, ensuring the appropriate distribution of team capacity across projects while maintaining balanced workloads and delivery efficiency.
  • Coordinate with management and stakeholders to monitor team capacity, utilization, and project delivery timelines.
  • Perform Jira Management: configure projects, workflows, boards, permissions, and issue types to align with team processes.
  • Manage Jira project settings, sprint boards, and backlogs to ensure accurate and up-to-date project tracking.
  • Conduct regular Jira Audits: Ensure projects are planned in Jira and everyone has tasks; review data integrity, workflow compliance, user access, and ensure reporting accuracy across projects.
  • Generate and publish regular Agile reports: including Sprint Reports, Velocity Charts, Burndown/Burnup Charts, and Cumulative Flow Diagrams — and submit them to the Leadership Team and concerned stakeholders in a timely manner.

Required Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of hands-on experience as a Scrum Master in a software development environment.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies — Scrum and Kanban.
  • Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or Professional Scrum Master (PSM I/II) — added advantage.
  • Proven experience facilitating Scrum events for multiple cross-functional teams.
  • Excellent interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Well-versed in Jira — including project configuration, board management, workflow customization, sprint tracking, reporting, and user administration.
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate complex organizational dynamics.
  • Experience working in a fast-paced, iterative software delivery environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advanced Certified Scrum Master (A-CSM) or Professional Scrum Master II (PSM II) certification.
  • Experience in managing and optimizing Kanban workflows for continuous delivery teams.
  • Hands-on experience with Jira Advanced Roadmaps, Jira automation rules, and dashboard creation for leadership reporting.
  • Background or exposure to software development, QA, or product management.
  • Experience in enterprise product companies.

Why You’ll Love Working With Us

  • Meaningful Work with Global Impact: Contribute to enterprise platforms used by world-class organizations such as Philips, Hitachi, Pfizer, and other global leaders—helping them improve how they operate, lead, and grow.
  • People-First, Collaborative Culture: Your ideas are heard, your work is valued, and you’re supported by a team built on trust and respect.
  • Purpose-Driven Technology: Build practical, thoughtful products that solve real operational and leadership challenges.
  • Growth & Ownership: Learn continuously, take ownership of your work, and grow your career in a supportive environment.
  • Global Exposure, Human Connection: Work with diverse teams and enterprise clients worldwide—while feeling connected and supported.
  • Quality, Security & Trust: Our platforms and processes are backed by internationally recognized ISO certifications, reflecting our strong commitment to quality, data security, and operational reliability. This means you work in an environment built on high standards and trust.

Build a Strong Future Together

At LTS, we don’t just deliver solutions—we build long-term relationships. Together, we create a strong, sustainable future for our people, our clients, and our organization. From continuous learning and career development opportunities to meaningful ownership in your work, we support you at every stage of your journey. Our people benefit from a supportive work environment, competitive benefits, and a culture that values balance, trust, and long-term success.

How to Apply

Interested candidates may forward their detailed resume to careers@ltslean.com with the subject "Application for Scrum Master" and include the following information in the email content: - Current CTC: - Expected CTC: - Notice Period: - Current Location: - Total Relevant Experience: - Willingness to work at office:

Closing Date: 14, Apr 2026 Job Published: 31, Mar 2026
summary: Lead Agile teams as a Scrum Master at LTS, a global Lean software company. Facilitate ceremonies, remove blockers, and ensure high-quality product delivery through collaboration and continuous improvement.
